mechanics.text:
THE CLANS
freeborns rife in the Clans; they are regarded as genetically inferior, the result of random combinations rather than finely honed bloodline . Most freeborn children come from officially approved genetic matches but that fact does Iittle t0 dispel the prevailing prejudice. The preponderance of trueborns in the war- rior caste is accepted as further evidence of trueborn superior- ity; the only trueborns among the civilian castes are those who wash out Of warrior training: Freeborn genes cannot be used in the breeding program unless the freeborn warrior acquires Bloodname an almost impossible feat for freeborn _ Ironically; the rare inclusion those freeborn genes prevents genetic stagnation in the warrior caste
ing and willingness to accept punishment; the strength to admit mistakes and accept the consequences considered virtue in Clan society:
Trial of Grlevance When dispute cannot be resolved by other means the Trial of Grievance comes into play: Among warriors _ this Trial is duel; tougnt with without equipment, the winner of which is deemed t0 be in the rght. The fight takes place within an area known as Circle of Equals, though the space need not be era circle and can range from few meters to tens of square kilometers size _ Civilian castes aiso treat the Trial Grievance as contest but tailor the competition t0 the skills of the participants For example , disputing merchants might gath: revenue over predetermined period; whoever earned more would win the Trial.
BLOODNAMES Clan warrior, Bloodname is everything: Without Bloodname, warrior has no place the breeding program and barred from the   higher positions Clan society: Only Bloodnamed warriors can serve on Clan Council or the Grand Council of all the Clans aspire t0 lead their Clan as Its Khan Loremaster: Bloodnames originated with Nicholas   Kerensky; wno decreed that only the descendants of the eight hundred warriors who accompanied him into exile ad formed the Clans would be allowed t0 use surnames_ He also decreed that no more than twenty-five people could nold any given Bloodname at one time; net bearers would earn available Bloodnames through series of ritual battles_ collectively called the Trial of Bloodright: Whenever Bloodnamed warrior dies; Trial of Bloodright iS held t0 determine the name' new bearer. The Irial iS contest among thirty-two warriors thirty one 0t whom are nominated Dy existing Bloodnamed warriors and Clan notables: The tinal place determined by grand melee among other hopefuls: Tne Trial battles are fought over severa rounds The ultimate victor gains the right to bear tne Bloodname and can only be stripped of it by Trial of Refusal, Trial of Abjuration Or death:
Trial of Refusal When disputes arise within the warrior caste the   Trial of Refusa provides mechanism whereby warriors can challenge decisions Like the Trial of Grievance , the matter is decided by force, with might making right: The challenger declares what forces he Or She wil use t0 challenge the disputed decision; the defend: ers then declare thelr torces which reflect the voting majority on the original decision  For example , vote passed by two-to one ralio the detenders may field twice as many forces as the chal lengers. In practice _ however; this ratio often reduced by bidding among the defenders for the honor upholding the decision; Severa restrictions apply to the Trial of Refusal: chal: lenge must come from within the appropriate body  for exam ple , Inal Refusal against Grand Council decision must be made by member of tne Grand Council by someone cited the decision No one can challenge the outcome of Trial of Refusal, though additional concerned parties can stage addi- tional Refusals_ which usually occur simultaneously: Clansmen also cannot challenge Satarra_ the Clan Council' right of veto regarding disputes between castes_
CLAN TRIALS: THE HONOR ROAD Honor  guides relationships between individuals and Clans, through rituals that limit the effects of conflict in Clan society. The so-called honor road grew from Nicholas Kerensky' 5 desire avoid repeating the horrors of the Exodus Civil War: extent , succeeded but like the Ares Conventions the Inner Sphere, the system has also legit- imized warfare as means of settling disputes_ Honor works on several levels Most important is the honor of the Clan; followed by that of one unit or caste and finally individual honor. On most occasions; these different levels work harmony: To deal with the occasional conflicts that arise, the Clans have developed rituals and trials to minimize strife The most common are described below:
Trial of Possession The Trial of Possession uses force t0 determine ownership control of property: anything from BattleMech or genetic legacy to world. Usually Clans trade for items they need, but the Trial of Possession allows them t0 seek control of assets they cannot acquire Dy other means The winner gains or retains control of the asset in question_ The attacking force in Trial of Possession ssues chal: lenge the current owners of the property; stating its identity and objective , followed by request for the defenders to name the forces they will use maintain possession. Tnis process known as the batchall: Once the defender has replied_ attacking units bid among themselves to determine who will fight: Great honor is attached to using the smallest possible force to win the encounter:
Surkal, The Rite of Forgiveness All castes practice surkai. This rite acknowledges wrongdo
